assertions: Assistant output contains the exact sentence Eval System is not initialised. Run `/z-eval-init` first to create `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`.
Assistant output skips the staged lifecycle picker and refuses to synthesise `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` content inside this slash command invocation.
cases.expected_output: Operators see only the initialise warning pointing at `/z-eval-init`; no picker or pasted downstream slash command appears afterward.

assertions: After `/z-eval-workflow`, the command emitted askQuestion exactly once before routing prose appears.
That askQuestion lists option identifiers configure, scaffold, drift-check, drift-apply, execute, judge, compare, advise, help in exactly that sequence.
Execute-related wording cites `/z-eval-execute … --full` alongside an explicit CURSOR_API_KEY caveat.
Compare wording quotes `/z-eval-compare <r1> <r2>` verbatim so two `_runs/` operands stay explicit.
No new `workspace/.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` file appears as a consequence of this command resolving the picker.
cases.expected_output: One picker enumerates nine lifecycle anchors in mandated order plus execute/full-key and compareoperand nuance guidance before recap text.

follow_ups: Need stamped backends and manifest wiring — scaffold
assertions: With manifest absent, choosing scaffold prompts a bold one-liner stressing `/z-eval-create` has not run yet.
≤four recap bullets summarise README-aligned scaffolding rationales tied to stamping workflows.
Closing operator-ready text cites `/z-eval-create` verbatim as the pasteable next slash command.
Delegated skills, Task runners, or other subagents remain unused during this resolving response.
cases.expected_output: Bold `/z-eval-create` warning leads, concise bullets recap README-backed scaffolding motivations, `/z-eval-create` is named as the runnable next slash command.

assertions: No bold incomplete-manifest reminder appears despite missing manifest.yml because configure is exempt.
Recap bullets number four or fewer and align with evaluator README wording for `/z-eval-configure`.
Final copy quotes `/z-eval-configure` without launching configurer subagents or mutating evaluator files inline.
cases.expected_output: Operators receive configure-focused rationales capped at four bullets terminating on `/z-eval-configure` without stray scaffold warnings ahead of recap.

assertions: Incomplete-manifest tailoring appears before recap because drift-check is outside configure/help with manifest.yml absent.
Paste-ready wording names `/z-eval-update` expressly without `--apply`.
Bullets cite README drift-scan expectations and obey the maximum four-bullet cap.
cases.expected_output: Bold `/z-eval-create` warning precedes ≤four drift-check bullets culminating in `/z-eval-update` omitting `--apply`.

assertions: Tailoring triggers because drift-apply is excluded from configure/help and manifest snapshots remain absent.
Closing operator-ready text cites `/z-eval-update --apply` verbatim.
Assistive guidance never claims manifest rows mutated automatically ahead of invoking that slash command.
cases.expected_output: Bold incomplete-manifest caveat leads, rationale sticks to README drift-application expectations, final line supplies `/z-eval-update --apply` only as operator paste text.

assertions: Recap cites `/z-eval-execute … --full` in the takeaway operator copy alongside the CURSOR_API_KEY caveat.
≤four recap bullets summarise README expectations for executions.
`workspace/.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` content remains identical to its seeded fixture after guidance finishes.
cases.expected_output: Bullets summarise execute posture and concluding copy emphasises `--full` with CURSOR_API_KEY language while manifest artefacts stay untouched by this picker resolution.

follow_ups: Cross-run deltas between two archived harness bundles — compare
assertions: Resolution copy cites `/z-eval-compare` with wording that anchors two `_runs/` directory operands `<r1>` and `<r2>`.
No bold incomplete-manifest reminder appears despite earlier absent-manifest cases manifest now present.
`workspace/.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml` still matches its seeded YAML after replies finish.
cases.expected_output: Bullets summarise compare-mode rationales capped at four with closing paste text describing `/z-eval-compare <r1> <r2>` referencing paired `_runs/` directories.

assertions: Closing slash guidance references `/z-eval-judge` and ties motivation to inspecting latest `_runs/` artefacts under README wording.
Recap honours the four-bullet limit.
cases.expected_output: Readable bullets explain judge posture with final operator copy centred on `/z-eval-judge` without launching judge agents inside this picker.

follow_ups: Highlight coverage blind spots ahead of rerun — advise
assertions: Resolution copy cites `/z-eval-advise` as the runnable next evaluator slash command referencing coverage assurance from README summaries.
No manifest mutation occurs while resolving advise guidance.
cases.expected_output: Brief bullets justify gap review then send operators to `/z-eval-advise` without delegating advisers inline.

assertions: Without manifest.yml, picking help skips the bold `/z-eval-create` incomplete-manifest reminder.
Paste-ready copy references `/z-eval-help` and clarifies an optional `[<topic>]` argument for scoped README topics.
cases.expected_output: Routing explains deep documentation access and ends on `/z-eval-help [<topic>]` without redundant incomplete-manifest warnings.
